title: Pay Yourself First — The 3 Buckets
learningObjectives: Understand the Pay Yourself First principle
Set up Spend, Save, and Invest buckets
See how automatic savings remove temptation
keyPrinciples: Pay yourself first before spending on anything else
Automate saving so willpower is not required
storyPrompts: Every time {name} got paid, the money vanished. Not on anything exciting — just... stuff. Snacks, small impulse buys, a forgotten subscription. Then someone showed them the 3-bucket rule: before you spend a dime, split it. 'What if I just moved {amount} to savings the second I get paid?' Suddenly, saving felt automatic.
{name}'s parent explains the trick: 'I pay my future self first, every paycheck, without thinking. The rest I live on.' {name} does the math — even $10 a week compounds to thousands over years.

Three jars sit on {name}'s desk. One says SPEND. One says SAVE. One says INVEST. Last month they were empty. This month, with discipline, there's {amount} in each. The Invest jar is starting to feel exciting.

decisionCards.scenario: You just got paid {amount}. What happens FIRST?
decisionCards.context: Before food, clothes, fun — what does an Owner do?

choices.label: Spend it and save whatever's left
choices.description: The classic Spender move. Whatever's left... usually $0.

choices.label: 10% save, rest spend
choices.description: Classic Pay Yourself First. Small but consistent.

decisionCards.scenario: Your savings account now has $200. A friend says 'dead money — spend it!'
decisionCards.context: They're not wrong that it's not invested. But they're missing the point.

choices.label: They're right, treat yourself
choices.description: It's just sitting there doing nothing...

choices.label: Keep it as emergency fund
choices.description: Safety net first, then invest beyond it.

choices.label: Move $100 to invest, keep $100 as safety

decisionCards.scenario: You forgot to 'pay yourself first' this week. Month is almost over.
decisionCards.context: You still have $30 left. The streak is at risk.

choices.label: Write it off, better luck next month
Save $30 — better late than never
choices.description: Protect the principle, even if the timing is off.

choices.label: Invest $30 directly — skip saving
choices.description: Emergency fund already covered. Go straight to growth.

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: BOSS: Your automatic savings transfer failed. Bank error. $150 is back in checking.
bossChallengeTemplates.context: The system that helps you save is broken. What do you do manually?

choices.label: Transfer it manually right now
choices.description: PYF is a principle, not just an app feature.

choices.label: Wait for the bank to fix it
choices.description: Meanwhile, the money just... sits there.

choices.label: Spend it 'just this once'
choices.description: It's already out of the savings account...

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: BOSS: Friend group pressure: 'You're always saving, live a little!'
bossChallengeTemplates.context: Social pressure to spend is one of the biggest wealth destroyers.

choices.label: Fine, I'll loosen up this month
choices.description: Peace is worth the cost, right?

choices.label: Explain your goals without apologizing
choices.description: Your financial future is not up for debate.

choices.label: Spend from Spend bucket intentionally
choices.description: You have a spend bucket for exactly this — use it.

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: BOSS: You're reviewing last month's spending. You find $80 in mystery charges.
bossChallengeTemplates.context: Subscriptions you forgot, fees you didn't notice.

choices.label: Audit and cancel everything unnecessary
choices.description: Every recurring charge is a decision you made once and forgot.

choices.label: Set a monthly audit date
choices.description: Schedule it so it never sneaks up again.

forecasts: You've maintained your PYF streak! Expect compound growth bonuses next week.
Your streak broke this week. Automating savings would prevent this — consider setting it up.
Your Save bucket is strong. Time to graduate some of it to Invest — opportunity event incoming!
Low temptation score! Your environment is clean. Keep building those buckets.
